questions.objective_ref: Create a dictionary of key-value pairs and access a value by its key, and explain why keys must be unique and hashable while values can be anything.
Create a dictionary of key-value pairs and access a value by its key, and explain why keys must be unique and hashable while values can be anything.
Create a dictionary of key-value pairs and access a value by its key (membership: in tests keys, not values).
Create a dictionary of key-value pairs and access a value by its key (bracket access raises KeyError on a missing key).
Add, modify, and delete entries with assignment (d[k] = v), del d[k], and d.pop(k), and describe what each does when the key is missing.
Add, modify, and delete entries with assignment (d[k] = v), del d[k], and d.pop(k), and describe what each does when the key is missing.
Add, modify, and delete entries with assignment (d[k] = v), del d[k], and d.pop(k), and describe what each does when the key is missing.
Loop over a dictionary's keys, values, and pairs using keys(), values(), and items().
Sort a dictionary's contents by key and by value using sorted() with a key function.
Sort a dictionary's contents by key and by value using sorted() with a key function.
Read and build nested dictionaries and access a value two levels deep.
Use get() and setdefault() to read or initialize a key safely without triggering a KeyError.
Write a dictionary comprehension to build a dictionary from an iterable, and to invert an existing dictionary (swap keys and values).
Use get() and setdefault() to read or initialize a key safely without triggering a KeyError.
Read and build nested dictionaries and access a value two levels deep.

objective_coverage: Create a dictionary and access a value by its key; keys unique + hashable, values anything (q1, q2, q3, q4)
Add, modify, and delete: d[k]=v, del d[k], d.pop(k[,default]) and missing-key behavior (q5, q6, q7)
Loop over keys(), values(), items() with tuple unpacking (q8)
Sort by key sorted(d) vs by value sorted(d.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1]) (q9, q10)
Read and build nested dictionaries; chained access d[outer][inner] (q11, q15)
get() vs setdefault() safe access; which one mutates (q12, q14)
Dictionary comprehension and inversion with the duplicate-values caveat (q13)
notes: Correct-answer answer-line order shuffled per MCQ with a uniform random shuffle seeded on the topic id ('5.2-dictionaries') for reproducibility. correct_position is the 0-based index of the = line in each MCQ answer block. Distractors encode corpus misconceptions: in tests values not keys (q3), bracket access silently returns None (q4), assignment adds a second entry for an existing key (q5), del returns the value / pop and del interchangeable (q6), pop always raises on missing key (q7), keys()/items() usable for unpacking pairs (q8), sorted(d) sorts values or reorders in place (q9, q10), sorting keys with kv[1] or values() with kv[1] (q10), setdefault does not mutate (q12), inverting with duplicate values keeps all entries or the first wins (q13).
